name: @webspinner-foundation/author
description: The authoring Spinner of the Warp build loop. Takes a brief — slug, display name, description, intent — and scaffolds a new Spinner bundle from a template, generates a Weaver's Tension scenario that exercises it, installs the bundle, and invokes Witness to verify the install end-to-end. Returns a structured report the Wizard reviews before merging. v0 is template-driven (no LLM); v1 adds Quiet Loom for intent → code translation.

capabilities.name: authorSpinner
capabilities.description: Scaffold a new Spinner from a template, generate a verifying scenario, install the bundle, and run Witness on the scenario. Returns a structured report the Wizard reviews. The closed loop: brief in, runnable-and-verified Spinner out.

slug.description: Kebab-case identifier. Becomes the directory name and the URL segment.

displayName.description: Human-readable name shown in the Skein and on the Spinner detail page.

description.description: One paragraph about what this Spinner does.

intent.description: Optional. A sentence of intent. v0 ignores this and uses the hello-spinner template; v1 will use it to drive LLM-assisted scaffold variations.

template.description: Template to scaffold from. v0 supports 'hello-spinner' only.

ok.description: True iff scaffold + install + scenario-generation + Witness all reported green.

scenarioSlug.description: The slug of the auto-generated scenario file at ~/warp/scenarios/<slug>-install.json.

bundlePath.description: Filesystem path to the scaffolded bundle (under ~/Cells/spinners/).

skeinName.description: The fully-qualified Spinner name (scope/slug) as registered in the Skein.

phase.description: How far the loop got. Useful for partial-failure diagnosis.

witnessReport.description: Witness's verifyScenario report on the generated scenario. Read this to see if the authored Spinner actually works.

pabloReport.description: Pablo's design-quality verdict on the rendered detail page of the just-authored Spinner. { verdict, verdict_text, in_pablo_voice, findings[] }. Concerns do not fail ok; only verdict='fails' does.
